name: Hoist 128x128 Hot-Band Shared Offsets Out Of The Steady-State Loop

summary.name: Hoist 128x128 Hot-Band Shared Offsets Out Of The Steady-State Loop
summary.hypothesis: Both active 128x128 hot-band kernels recompute warp-local A-row and B-column shared offsets inside the K steady-state loop even though those offsets are invariant for the entire CTA. Hoisting those offsets once per warp should shave integer address work and reduce control dilution on a path where tensor activity is only 48.37 percent while DRAM is far from saturated.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared-pointer arithmetic and loop-carried control overhead in the 128x128 hot-band steady state are stealing issue slots from tensor work.
summary.risk: Low. The change is local, preserves the same tile geometry, and only hoists invariant offset math.
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest way to test whether the current gap versus the best measured run is coming from hot-loop control overhead instead of macro geometry.
It touches both active 128x128 hot-band variants without reopening the old restore-or-replay path.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the implementation needs to change CTA geometry, cp.async stage count, or the dispatch map instead of remaining a local offset-hoist cleanup.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
